title: record_fact
description: Persist a typed-edge fact (subject, predicate, object) into the local cognitive graph and return the stored fact id. Entities referenced by name are auto-created if they do not exist. Predicate is drawn from a controlled vocabulary in v0.1.0; source and confidence are optional declarative metadata.

subject.description: The subject of the fact. If id is provided, it is used; otherwise the (type, name) pair is upserted.

predicate.description: Edge type drawn from the v0.1.0 controlled vocabulary. Extensible later via type_extensions.

object.description: The object of the fact. Use the literal form for tag values, status strings, or free-text notes where the target is a value rather than an entity.

source.description: Optional free-text origin (URL, message id, transcript citation). Stored verbatim; never fetched. Null or absent means undeclared.

confidence.description: Declared confidence in the fact, 0..1. Defaults to 1.0 when omitted (declarative). Callers SHOULD lower this for inferred facts.

fact_id.description: Stable canonical id for the stored fact. Opaque to callers.

recorded_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset for when the fact was committed.

subject.description: Resolved subject (with id populated) as stored.

deduplicated.description: True when an existing near-identical fact was matched and reused instead of inserting a new row. Dedup strategy is server-internal in v0.1.0 (see ADR 0002 open question).

auto_created_entities.description: Entities that were created as a side effect of this call because they did not previously exist. Empty array when no creation occurred.

SUBJECT_REQUIRED.description: The subject argument was missing or did not include at least one of {id, name}.

OBJECT_REQUIRED.description: The object argument was missing or did not include at least one of {id, name, literal}.

PREDICATE_UNKNOWN.description: Predicate is not in the v0.1.0 controlled vocabulary. Use one of: mentioned_in, decided_in, reports_to, depends_on, resolved_by, blocked_by, tagged, belongs_to. Extension predicates land in v0.2.

ENTITY_TYPE_UNKNOWN.description: Subject or object referenced an entity type not in the v0.1.0 taxonomy.

CONFIDENCE_OUT_OF_RANGE.description: Confidence was not in the closed interval [0, 1].

GRAPH_WRITE_FAILED.description: Local SQLite store rejected the write (disk full, locked, or corrupted). Caller MAY retry once; persistent failure requires user action.

examples.name: record a decision attribution with a slack source
tag a project with a free-text literal
duplicate fact is deduplicated, not inserted

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.1.x. New predicates and entity types land via the v0.2 type_extensions mechanism, not by mutating this schema. Renaming or retyping any field requires a new major version with a parallel $id.
compatibility.side_effects: Mutates the local cognitive graph. Auto-creates entities by (type, name) when they do not exist. Idempotent under the dedup policy: recording the same (subject, predicate, object) twice returns the same fact_id with deduplicated=true on the second call.
compatibility.idempotency: Idempotent at the (subject, predicate, object) granularity. Source and confidence on a duplicate call are merged server-side per a documented policy (see ADR 0002 open question).
compatibility.privacy: Writes only to the local SQLite store. No remote calls. Source strings (which may be URLs or message ids) are sensitive: they reveal where the user was when they observed the fact. Stored verbatim, never fetched, never transmitted off-device by this server.

type.description: Entity type from the v0.1.0 taxonomy.

id.description: Canonical id if known. When omitted, the (type, name) pair is used to upsert.

name.description: Display name. Required when id is not provided.

literal.description: Free-text value used when the fact's object is not an entity (e.g. a tag, a status, a short note).
